headline: Entergy Louisiana announces a new agreement with Meta that will deliver an additional $2B in customer savings
summary: Entergy Louisiana today announced an additional agreement with Meta to support the hyperscale data center in Northeast Louisiana. Structured to ensure Meta pays its full cost of service, the agreement is expected to deliver approximately $2 billion in customer savings to Entergy Louisiana customers over 20 years, in addition to the $650 million previously announced.

content.title: Entergy Louisiana announces a new agreement with Meta that will deliver an additional $2B in customer savings
content.summary: Entergy Louisiana today announced an additional agreement with Meta to support the hyperscale data center in Northeast Louisiana. headline: Utility Entergy says revised Meta data-center deal to deliver higher customer savings
summary: Utility Entergy said Meta Platforms will pay its full cost of ‌service for the planned hyperscale data ‌center in northeast Louisiana under a revised agreement.  Meta had disclosed plans to ⁠invest about $10  billion in a hyperscale data center in Richland Parish, northeast Louisiana, a project the ​Facebook parent ​unveiled in late 2024.  Big ​technology companies are ‌increasingly partnering with utilities to power the rapid expansion of data centers to cater to the surging demand for artificial intelligence tools and cloud computing.
